question: Does the current environment/circumstances builder in userspace.js violate the general cognitive substrate boundary, and if so, what is the smallest foundational refactor that preserves the useful behavior while moving Akash/filesystem-specific inference into a better layer?
notes: The design standard is elegance, simplicity, generality, and low LOC. Environment-shaped logic should not leak into the general cognitive substrate unless it is clearly part of cognition itself.
The current userspace.js circumstances builder includes path regexes, prose-to-path inference, self-maintenance hint lists, and explicit filesystem/project-ish heuristics.
Proto-DR-2 already surfaced one concrete failure mode here: candidate-surface inference from prose produced bogus paths.
The question is deeper than that one bug. The question is whether this entire class of logic belongs in userspace at all, or in some observation/world-model adapter that userspace consumes.
Please prefer subtractive or boundary-clarifying solutions over additive schema unless a new field is unavoidable.
